channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: UAE Minister Praises PM Modi Ahead of Bilateral Visit
item.description: Ahead of Prime Minister Narendra Modi's five-nation tour starting with the United Arab Emirates, UAE Minister of State for International Cooperation, Reem Al Hashimy, commended PM Modi as a 'true trea...

item.title: Kenya Parliament Questions Lack of Funding for Key Tourism Agencies
item.description: Members of Kenya's National Assembly have expressed serious concern over the absence of budget allocations for major tourism agencies in the proposed 2026/27 national budget. During a meeting chaired ...

item.title: Important Guidelines for Passengers, Drivers and Cyclists on Tanzanian Roads
item.description: In Mwanza, the Regional Traffic Officer (RTO), Mr Sunday Ibrahim, gave detailed advice on road safety focusing on passengers, bus capacities, driver conduct, and cyclists. Passengers are advised to pl...

item.title: Health Department Plans Major Investments in Hospital Infrastructure
item.description: The South African Department of Health is set to submit over ten bids to the expanded Budget Facility for Infrastructure (BFI) starting in July 2026. Health Minister Dr Aaron Motsoaledi outlined that ...

item.title: Trump demonstrates unusual deference to Xi during Beijing summit
item.description: During his summit in Beijing, US President Donald Trump displayed an unusually deferential and cautious demeanour towards Chinese President Xi Jinping. The first day of meetings saw Trump impressed by...

item.title: Investigation Reveals No Road Construction at Suleja Market Despite N1.5 Billion Claimed Spending
item.description: For Suleja International Market wey dey Rafinseyi area, Niger State Government don claim say dem spend N1.514 billion on road construction inside the market. But investigation wey SaharaReporters run ...

item.title: Governments urged to improve protection against frequent heatwaves
item.description: Heatwaves have become a recurring and severe threat to human health, livelihoods, and wellbeing, particularly across Europe and globally. In the summer of 2025, a ten-day heatwave in 12 major European...

item.title: Alameda County settles wrongful death lawsuit for $36 million after deputy’s crimes
item.description: Alameda County has agreed to pay $36 million to the family of Benison and Maria Tran after a wrongful death lawsuit stemming from the September 2022 killings by off-duty Deputy Devin Williams. The inc...

item.title: Access Issue on Monash Weather Page
item.description: The Herald Sun's Monash weather page is currently inaccessible for some users due to automated traffic management systems that detect and block crawler bots. If you are a regular reader or subscriber ...

item.title: VD Satheesan Scheduled to Assume Office as Kerala Chief Minister on 18th May
item.description: Due to restricted access, the full content of the news article could not be retrieved from NDTV. However, the headline indicates that VD Satheesan is set to be sworn in as the Chief Minister of Kerala...

item.title: Don Julio celebrates African talent at AMVCA through immersive experiences
item.description: At the 12th Africa Magic Viewers’ Choice Awards (AMVCA), Don Julio no be just any sponsor, e show say e understand African culture well well. The brand create The Don’s Table, special space wey celebr...

item.title: Bonifacio Drive in Manila reopens after drainage improvements
item.description: Bonifacio Drive, one of Manila's key roads, is now open to traffic following the completion of essential drainage upgrades. The Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) announced that the road r...

item.title: Eddie Murphy’s Early Years on Saturday Night Live and His Lasting Impact
item.description: Eddie Murphy joined Saturday Night Live at just 19 years old, during a period when the show was struggling after the departure of its original cast and creator, Lorne Michaels, in 1980. His arrival re...

item.title: Tucson prepares to host Iran's World Cup team amid Middle East conflict
item.description: Tucson, Arizona, is getting ready to host Iran's national football team for the FIFA World Cup 2026, despite ongoing tensions in the Middle East. Preparations at the Kino Sports Complex include ensuri...

item.title: Tanzania Yet to Follow EAC Court Ruling on Kenyan Match Imports
item.description: Tanzania imekabiliwa na lawama baada ya kudaiwa kushindwa kutekeleza amri iliyotolewa na Mahakama ya Umoja wa Afrika Mashariki (EACJ) kuzuia kodi ya ziada ya sh 400 kwa kilo kwenye mpira wa usalama un...

item.title: TMRC and Habitat for Humanity Introduce Microfinance Housing Loans for Low-Income Tanzanians
item.description: The Tanzania Mortgage Refinance Company (TMRC), working with Habitat for Humanity International, just launched new housing microfinance products to help poor families, especially those working in info...

item.title: Government to Make Civil Servants’ Asset Declarations Public in Redacted Format to Ensure Transparency
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The government has announced that asset declarations of civil servants, as required under IMF governance and anti-corruption benchmarks, will now be made publicly accessible in a redacted f...

item.title: Understanding Group Dynamics and Workplace Pressure in Team Decisions
item.description: A workplace contributor shared their experience of colleagues turning against them during a meeting, attributing it to the influence of a powerful and vindictive personality. Associate Professor Conni...

item.title: Labour’s Achievements and the Need for Bold Leadership Change
item.description: Polly Toynbee argues that while the Labour Party under Keir Starmer has implemented significant reforms, such as improvements to child poverty, education, the NHS, workers’ rights, and green energy in...

item.title: Vigilante Anti-Immigration Marches in South Africa Raise Legal and Safety Concerns
item.description: In recent months, South Africa has seen a surge in marches organised by groups campaigning against so-called 'illegal immigration' and crime, with events drawing large crowds in cities such as Durban,...
